Think:
The user is interested in exercises for leg strength. I should recommend a few exercises that target the quadriceps, hamstrings, and glutes. Since the user didn't specify any preferences, I'll provide a general explanation.

Textual response:
For leg strength, I recommend the following exercises:

1. Squats: This exercise targets the quadriceps, hamstrings, and glutes. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, then bend your knees and lower your body down until your thighs are parallel to the ground. Push back up to the starting position.
2. Lunges: This exercise targets the quadriceps, hamstrings, and glutes. Stand with your feet together, take a large step forward with one foot, and lower your body down until your back knee almost touches the ground. Push back up to the starting position and repeat with the other leg.
3. Deadlifts: This exercise targets the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, bend down and grab a weight or barbell with your hands shoulder-width apart, and lift the weight up to hip level. Lower the weight back down to the starting position.

These exercises will help you build strength in your legs, but remember to start with lighter weights and gradually increase the load as you become more comfortable with the movements.

Think:
The user wants to know how to perform each exercise safely and correctly. I should provide a step-by-step guide for each exercise.

Textual response:
Here's how to perform each exercise safely and correctly:

• Squats:
  - Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
  - Keep your back straight and your core engaged.
  - Lower your body down until your thighs are parallel to the ground.
  - Push back up to the starting position.
  - Keep your weight in your heels and avoid letting your knees extend past your toes.

• Lunges:
  - Stand with your feet together.
  - Take a large step forward with one foot.
  - Lower your body down until your back knee almost touches the ground.
  - Push back up to the starting position.
  - Alternate legs with each repetition.

• Deadlifts:
  - Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
  - Bend down and grab a weight or barbell with your hands shoulder-width apart.
  - Lift the weight up to hip level, keeping your back straight and your core engaged.
  - Lower the weight back down to the starting position.
  - Avoid rounding your back or letting the weight swing out of control.

Textual response:
Here's how each exercise affects different leg muscles:

• Squats:
  - Quadriceps: The quadriceps muscles in the front of the thigh are worked during squats.
  - Hamstrings: The hamstrings muscles in the back of the thigh are also worked during squats.
  - Glutes: The glutes muscles in the buttocks are worked during squats.

• Lunges:
  - Quadriceps: The quadriceps muscles in the front of the thigh are worked during lunges.
  - Hamstrings: The hamstrings muscles in the back of the thigh are also worked during lunges.
  - Glutes: The glutes muscles in the buttocks are worked during lunges.

• Deadlifts:
  - Glutes: The glutes muscles in the buttocks are worked during deadlifts.
  - Hamstrings: The hamstrings muscles in the back of the thigh are also worked during deadlifts.
  - Quadriceps: The quadriceps muscles in the front of the thigh are worked during deadlifts.

These exercises work multiple muscle groups in the legs, making them effective for building overall leg strength and muscle mass.
